name: Pete Rugolo
description: American jazz composer, arranger, and record producer (1915–2011)
abstract: Pietro Rugolo (December 25, 1915 – October 16, 2011), known professionally as Pete Rugolo, was an American jazz composer, arranger, and record producer.

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Poston
about_quotes.evidence: when we would do a concert that had something to do with Rugolo, I would be in his garage for two or three days finding all these original scores... enabled us to be able to do the events and present all these things
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Poston interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...gave a lot of good writers their break like Holman or Lenny Niehaus, or Rugolo, or Graettinger... [INTERVIEWEE] I had some kids by then... I was with that band for about a year and a half...
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mario Bauza
about_quotes.evidence: So he came to the Palladium with Pete Rugolo ... to listen to the band. ... And then in (inaudible) to that he had Pete Rugolo wrote a tune called (inaudible) Machito. That was how -- how we got acquainted.

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Pete Rugolo wrote pieces played on a session with JJ Johnson, but JJ Johnson does not mention Rugolo or describe any personal or professional relationship with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: And in the meantime, I was doing all the rest of this studio work -- jingles and record dates and Nelson Riddle and Billy May and you name it, with everybody...even Pete Rugolo.

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: Pete Rugolo arranging and conducting on this one... Was that a rewarding and a memorable experience, working for Terry? ... Yeah. Later on, when he got his own band
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
